perturbation.original_text: Profit and Loss Subject to any other provisions of this Agreement, the net profits and losses of the Joint Venture, for both accounting and tax purposes, will accrue to and be borne by the Participants in proportion to the Participants' Capital Contributions inclusive of any Additional Capital Contributions (the "Profit and Loss Distribution").
perturbation.changed_text: Profit and Loss Subject to any other provisions of this Agreement, the net profits and losses of the Joint Venture, for both accounting and tax purposes, will accrue to and be borne by the Participants as decided by a majority vote.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'in proportion to the Participants' Capital Contributions inclusive of any Additional Capital Contributions (the "Profit and Loss Distribution")' the determination of profit and loss is changed from a fixed proportion to a discretionary majority vote. This creates an in-text contradiction because subsequent clauses refer to the 'Dissolution Distribution' (which is directly linked to the Profit and Loss Distribution based on capital contributions), implying a pre-determined allocation. This creates uncertainty in determining the appropriate calculation method for dissolution.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distribution of Property on Dissolution of Joint Venture In the event of the dissolution of the Joint Venture, each Participant will share in any remaining assets or liabilities of the Joint Venture in proportion to the Participants' Capital Contributions inclusive of any Additional Capital Contributions (the "Dissolution Distribution").
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the predetermined allocation of profit and loss based on capital contributions, replacing it with a majority vote. This directly contradicts the 'Dissolution Distribution' clause, which specifies that assets are distributed in proportion to capital contributions. The modification introduces uncertainty and a clear conflict regarding how profits and assets are to be allocated, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Actions Requiring Unanimous Consent of the Participants The following list of actions will require the unanimous consent of all Participants: committing the Joint Venture to new liabilities or obligations totaling To Be Determined; and incurring single expenditures that exceed "To Be Determined".
perturbation.changed_text: Actions Requiring Unanimous Consent of the Participants The following list of actions will require the unanimous consent of all Participants: committing the Joint Venture to new liabilities or obligations totaling ; and incurring single expenditures that exceed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'To Be Determined' from the section outlining the actions requiring unanimous consent of the participants, specifically concerning new liabilities or obligations and single expenditures, the contract now lacks clear thresholds for these actions. This creates an internal contradiction, because, this omission introduces ambiguity, as any expenditure or liability, regardless of its size, could be argued to require unanimous consent, conflicting with other sections that grant management authority to a majority vote.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Management Except as all of the Participants may otherwise agree in writing, all actions and decisions respecting the management, operation and control of the Joint Venture and its business will be decided by a majority vote of the Participants.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that unanimous consent was required for actions 'totaling To Be Determined' and 'exceeding To Be Determined'. The changed text removes 'To Be Determined', creating ambiguity regarding the thresholds for actions requiring unanimous consent. This directly contradicts the clause stating that management decisions are made by a majority vote, as any expenditure or liability, regardless of amount, could now be argued to require unanimous consent, overriding the majority vote provision.

perturbation.original_text: A withdrawing Part ic ipant 's interest wi l l be based on that Participant's proportion of the Dissolution Distribution described above, less any outstanding liabilities the withdrawing Participant may have to the Joint Venture. The intent of this section is to ensure the survival of the Joint Venture despite the withdrawal of any individual Participant.
perturbation.changed_text: A withdrawing Part ic ipant 's interest wi l l be based on an amount to be determined at the time of withdrawal, less any outstanding liabilities the withdrawing Participant may have to the Joint Venture. The intent of this section is to ensure the survival of the Joint Venture despite the withdrawal of any individual Participant.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clear basis for valuing a withdrawing participant's interest, namely 'that Participant's proportion of the Dissolution Distribution described above,' and replacing it with the ambiguous phrase 'an amount to be determined at the time of withdrawal,' the contract creates an internal contradiction. This change makes the distribution process arbitrary. This is contradicting because the same agreement still mentions Dissolution Distribution, creating in-text contradition and making the enforcement ambiguous.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Distribution of Property on Dissolution of Joint Venture In the event of the dissolution of the Joint Venture, each Participant will share in any remaining assets or liabilities of the Joint Venture in proportion to the Participants' Capital Contributions inclusive of any Additional Capital Contributions (the "Dissolution Distribution").
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces a clear valuation method for a withdrawing participant's interest ('that Participant's proportion of the Dissolution Distribution described above') with an ambiguous 'amount to be determined at the time of withdrawal.' This directly contradicts the section stating that 'each Participant will share in any remaining assets or liabilities of the Joint Venture in proportion to the Participants' Capital Contributions inclusive of any Additional Capital Contributions (the "Dissolution Distribution").' By removing the link to the Dissolution Distribution for withdrawing participants, the contract introduces a conflicting method for determining their share, creating an in-text contradiction under the 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.
